commentaries.author: Ambrosiaster
commentaries.content: It is a stumbling block to the Jews when they hear Christ calling himself the Son of God yet not observing the sabbath. It is foolishness to the Gentiles because they hear things like the virgin birth and the resurrection being preached but regard them as irrational. Commentary on Paul’s Epistles.

commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: Into the Jews a stumbling-block, and unto the Greeks foolishness. Notice here, with S. Chrysostom (Hom. moral in loco, and above on ver17), that the power of the Cross shines forth not only in itself but also in its preaching: (1. ) In the fact that the Apostles, few in number, simple fishermen, poor, unlearned, unknown, and Jews, in all these respects hateful to the world, yet brought the world into subjection to the Cross. ) In the fact that they subdued most bitter enemies, demons, sin, death, hell, kings, princes, philosophers, orators, Greeks, barbarians, laws, judgments. Long-existing religions, and time-honoured traditions. ) In that they persuaded men by simple preaching, and not by arms, Wisdom of Solomon , or eloquence. ) In that in so short a time they spread the faith of Christ over the whole world. ) In that by the grace of Christ they overcame most cheerfully and courageously what is hardest to be borne by the natural strength of Prayer of Manasseh , the threats of tyrants, scourgings, deaths, and tortures. ) In that they preached a doctrine not about a glorious God, but a crucified One, and Him their Saviour to be believed in and adored; and a law of Christ displeasing to nature and flesh. Wherefore Tertullian (lib. ) beautifully and fitly compares the Kingdom of Christ with the kingdoms of all kings and people, and prefers it before them all: " Song of Solomon ," he says, "reigned, but only in the borders of Judæa from Dan to Beersheba: Darius reigned over the Babylonians and Parthians, but not further; Pharaoh reigned over the Egyptians, but over them only. The kingdom of Nebuchadnezzar stretched only from India to Ethiopia. Alexander of Macedon, after subduing all Asia and other countries, could not keep what he had conquered. So have the Germans, Britons, Moors, and Romans bounds set to their dominions. But the kingdom of Christ has reached to all parts, His name is believed on everywhere, is worshipped by all nations, everywhere reigns, is everywhere adored; He is equal to all, King over all, Judge over all, God and Lord of all.

commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content: And again, writing to the Corinthians, he declares, "But we preach Christ Jesus crucified; "

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content:
Of the passion itself should be figuratively set forth in predictions; and the more incredible (that mystery), the more likely to be "a stumbling-stone".
Are in the hands of Him who gave us the Scriptures which we use, it follows that the apostle, when treating of the Creator, (as Him whom both Jew and Gentile as yet have) not known, means undoubtedly to teach us, that the God who is to become known (in Christ) is the Creator. The very "stumbling-block "which he declares Christ to be "to the Jews"
